public_functions.signature: void initGtmTomPwm(float32 dutyA, float32 dutyB, float32 dutyC)
public_functions.behavior_description: Enable the GTM module and enable the GTM CMU clocks required by the TOM time base. Initialize a TOM time-base driver for the specified TOM instance and the designated time-base channel to achieve a 20 kHz PWM period using the default GTM clock source. Prepare a complementary high/low PWM driver configuration with three high/low channel pairs, assign the output pins via the generic GTM PinMap using push-pull output mode and an automotive-grade pad driver, and set both high-side and low-side active states to active-high. Channel-to-pin mapping used for this module: - TOM1_CH0 (Phase A high) -> TOUT0 on P02.0 - TOM1_CH1 (Phase A low) -> TOUT1 on P02.1 - TOM1_CH2 (Phase B high) -> TOUT2 on P02.2 - TOM1_CH3 (Phase B low) -> TOUT3 on P02.3 - TOM1_CH4 (Phase C high) -> TOUT4 on P02.4 - TOM1_CH5 (Phase C low) -> TOUT5 on P02.5. Configure PWM parameters: center-aligned mode, deadtime of 0.5 microseconds, and minimum pulse of 1.0 microseconds. Initialize the PWMHL driver with the timer as its time base. Compute initial on-time ticks for each phase from the timer period using the duty fractions provided as function parameters (dutyA, dutyB, dutyC), expressed as 0.0 to 1.0 fractions (e.g., 0.25f, 0.50f, 0.75f). Apply the initial on-times coherently using a synchronous update sequence: disableUpdate on the timer, set the on-times for the three channels in the PWMHL driver, then applyUpdate on the timer so all updates are latched simultaneously.

Fetch the current timer period from the PWMHL driver's linked timer. Compute a duty step equal to 10% of the period, along with minimum and maximum on-time thresholds corresponding to 0% and 100% of the period. For each of the three phases, add the duty step to the stored on-time value. If any on-time meets or exceeds the maximum threshold, wrap it to the minimum threshold to maintain a 0–100% range. Apply all three updated on-times coherently by surrounding the PWMHL on-time update with a synchronous sequence: first disableUpdate on the timer, then set all three on-times in the PWMHL driver, and finally applyUpdate on the timer to latch the new values simultaneously.

header_selection.reconciliation_reason: LLM driver_calls use IfxGtm_Tom_* API, not IfxGtm_Tom_PwmHl (the reference API). Updated to match actual usage.
